skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with intentional aesthetics, high craft, and non-generic visual identity. Use when building or styling web UIs, components, pages, dashboards, or frontend applications.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents to design polished, memorable frontend interfaces with working implementation details.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported network, external command, reconnaissance, and weak cryptography patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from Apache License text, markdown prose, and a fenced arithmetic formula, with no executable code or prompt injection found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Apache License Text Flagged as Risky Code
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL and weak cryptography findings in LICENSE.txt are standard Apache License 2.0 legal text, not executable network or cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are part of the unmodified Apache License 2.0 terms. They do not define code, commands, secrets, network calls, or cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Formula Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick findings are markdown code-fence delimiters around a DFII scoring formula. They do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is a fenced markdown block containing an arithmetic expression. No shell command, interpreter invocation, or user-controlled execution path exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Prose Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings in SKILL.md are natural-language design instructions and headings. They do not reference hash functions, ciphers, or cryptographic APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain prose such as headings, design requirements, and checklist items. No evidence of cryptographic implementation or unsafe algorithm selection was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Guidance Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings are ordinary frontend design guidance about avoiding default patterns, system fonts, balanced palettes, grid choices, and motion.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are aesthetic rules, not host inspection or environment discovery. No commands or data collection instructions are present.

content.value_statement: Generic AI-generated interfaces often look interchangeable and lack product fit. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward memorable UI concepts with practical implementation standards.
actual_capabilities: Defines a clear aesthetic direction before implementation starts.
Uses a design feasibility and impact score to evaluate visual concepts.
Guides typography, color, spacing, motion, texture, and layout decisions.
Requests working HTML, CSS, JavaScript, or framework code instead of static mockups.
Adds accessibility, performance, and maintainability checks to frontend output.
Identifies anti-patterns such as default fonts, generic gradients, and template-like layouts.
limitations: It provides design guidance and does not include executable implementation files.
It cannot validate visual output without the host agent running previews or tests.
It may need product context to choose an appropriate aesthetic direction.
It does not supply reusable component libraries or packaged assets.

use_cases.title: Create a Product Landing Page
use_cases.description: Turn a product brief into a memorable landing page with a clear visual thesis and working frontend code.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Dashboard
use_cases.description: Replace generic layout choices with a focused design system that supports repeated operational use.

use_cases.title: Build a Portfolio Experience
use_cases.description: Create a distinct visual identity that makes projects feel curated, polished, and easy to scan.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to create a single responsive page for my product. Define the aesthetic direction, explain the design system, and provide working implementation guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Redesign a Generic Interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for improving an existing UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to redesign this interface so it feels less generic. Keep the core workflow, but improve typography, layout, color, motion, and visual identity.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Design System Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for scalable product design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to define a frontend design direction for a multi-page product. Include DFII scoring, component principles, spacing rhythm, color variables, and accessibility constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Build a High-Craft Prototype
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to produce an implementation-ready prototype. Include the design direction summary, system snapshot, differentiation callout, and production-quality frontend code.

output_examples.input: Design a landing page for a developer analytics tool.
output: A design direction named industrial observatory with a strong technical tone.
A compact design system covering display type, neutral surfaces, sharp accents, and restrained motion.
A working page plan with hero, data preview, feature flow, and conversion areas.

output_examples.input: Make this SaaS dashboard feel more distinctive.
output: A diagnosis of generic layout patterns that weaken the interface.
A revised information hierarchy with denser scanning areas and clearer state treatment.
A visual system that avoids default fonts, predictable cards, and common gradient treatments.

output_examples.input: Create a portfolio homepage for a motion designer.
output: A memorable aesthetic anchor tied to the creator's work and audience.
Guidance for typography, pacing, preview modules, hover states, and mobile composition.
A final checklist for accessibility, performance, and visual consistency.

best_practices: Provide audience, product purpose, brand constraints, and technical stack before asking for implementation.
Ask for the DFII score and revise low-scoring directions before writing code.
Preview the generated interface across screen sizes and check accessibility before shipping.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without product context or audience context.
Accepting decorative effects that do not support the stated aesthetic direction.
Skipping implementation checks after receiving visually ambitious frontend code.

faq.question: Does this skill write frontend code?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the agent to produce working frontend implementation guidance, not only visual descriptions.

faq.question: Can it work with React projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes React guidance and favors functional components with composable styling.

faq.question: Does it include a component library?
faq.answer: No. It is an instruction skill and does not ship reusable components or packaged assets.

faq.question: Is it only for landing pages?
faq.answer: No. It can guide pages, dashboards, components, portfolios, and frontend applications.

faq.question: How does it avoid generic AI design?
faq.answer: It requires a named aesthetic direction, a differentiation anchor, and checks against common template-like patterns.

faq.question: Should generated UI still be reviewed?
faq.answer: Yes. Review layout, accessibility, performance, and brand fit before using generated frontend work in production.
